title: 10 best herbal creams for ringworm

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Tea tree oil's potent antifungal properties help combat the growth and spread of fungal organisms that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton, Epidermophyton, and Microsporum.
Antibacterial activity: Additionally, tea tree oil's antibacterial properties can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may arise from ringworm lesions or compromised skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a more comfortable and faster healing process.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to effectively combat fungal infections, including ringworm. When applied topically, tea tree oil cream can help to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritation, and inhibit the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm. Its antibacterial properties also help to prevent secondary infections, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of scarring. As a result, tea tree oil cream has become a popular natural remedy for ringworm treatment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This terpene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help to inhibit the growth of fungi causing ringworm, thereby promoting its healing.
Cymene: This monoterpene exhibits antifungal activity, which aids in the reduction of fungal infections and the resolution of symptoms associated with ringworm.
Terpinen-4-ol: This terpene has potent antifungal properties that help to inhibit the proliferation of fungi, facilitate the healing process, and reduce inflammation associated with ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties that help combat ringworm.
Seeds: Not much information is known about the seeds, however seeds are sometimes used in herbal remedies.
Roots: The roots of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can aid in treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at peak oil content and dry them using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 8 hours.
Weigh 20g of the dried leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 50g of beeswax, 20g of candelilla wax, and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt at 60-70°c for 30 minutes.
Add the ground plant material to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine, then remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections, providing relief to affected skin.

Antifungal property: The antifungal agents present in aloe vera, such as aloin and acemannan, combat fungal growth and inhibit the spread of ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of recurrence.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera's rich concentration of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids accelerates wound closure by stimulating collagen production, improving skin elasticity, and enhancing tissue repair, which is particularly beneficial in treating ringworm lesions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal infections, including ringworm. Additionally, aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects help to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and healing process. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a anthraquinone compound, has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which can help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid glycoside, possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, allowing them to help combat fungal infections such as ringworm by disrupting the fungal cell membrane.
Vitamins E and C: Vitamins E and C, antioxidants present in Aloe vera, can help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery from ringworm infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of aloe vera's active compounds, including glycerin, vitamins, and minerals, which soothe and heal skin irritations.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in ringworm.
Sap from Leaves: The sap or juice of the Aloe vera leaves contains a combination of enzymes, vitamins, and minerals that promote skin healing and reduce redness associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize your equipment and workspace by washing with soap and water then sanitizing with alcohol for 5 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of aloe vera gel with 50 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of preservative then mix well.
Pour the mixture into sterile jars and allow it to cool and set for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Neem's antifungal compounds, such as nimbin and azadirachtin, inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, effectively treating ringworm by targeting the underlying cause.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Neem's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and relieving discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Neem's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage caused by ringworm, helping to prevent scarring and promote healthy tissue regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica, also known as neem, creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in neem, such as azadirachtin and nimbin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm. These compounds also promote healing and reduce inflammation, making neem creams an effective natural remedy for treating ringworm. remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have potent antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of ringworm-causing fungi such as Trichophyton and Microsporum.
Azadirachtin: This alkaloid has antifungal, antibacterial, and antiparasitic properties, making it effective in treating ringworm by preventing fungal spore germination and suppressing fungal growth.

remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of azadirachtin, a compound that exhibits antifungal properties.

Barks: They are used due to their content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of azadirachta indica leaves and 10 grams of its roots using 500 ml of neutral oil for 2 hours.
Filter the oil extract through a coffee filter to remove impurities and sediment for 30 minutes.
Combine the filtered oil extract with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70-80 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to melt the waxes and blend the ingredients.
Pour the hot mixture into a mold and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Calendula, the key compound found in pot marigold cream, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms such as Candida albicans, which can cause ringworm, by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from producing toxins.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in pot marigold cream have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and minimizing the risk of infection spreading.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula has been traditionally used to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and enhancing fibroblast proliferation, which helps accelerate the process of resolving ringworm lesions.

Calendula's natural soothing properties also help to reduce itching, redness, and inflammation, promoting a healthy and balanced environment for skin healing. Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis have been shown to possess antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause ringworm and promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery.

Nascent oxygen: Released from the action of saponins in Calendula officinalis, nascent oxygen helps to kill fungal spores and bacteria that cause ringworm, promoting a clean and healthy environment for skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for ringworm due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for ringworm due to their antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for ringworm due to their antifungal properties, which help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh calendula flowers on a sunny day when in full bloom.
Infuse 2 cups of calendula flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil at 6 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container at 30 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter at 10 minutes.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and pour it into a tin container for storage at 20 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice creams contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antifungal properties: Licorice root extract has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, including those that cause ringworm, making it a potential treatment for the infection.

The active compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as glycyrrhizin and saponins, inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm. Flavonoids (specifically, luteolin and isoorientin): These polyphenolic compounds have antifungal and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and fungal infections that cause ringworm.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots dried in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 100g of powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 500g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil melt.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and let it cool to room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of olea europaea leaves and 20g of olea europaea flowers on a sunny day at 10 am.
Infuse 50g of olea europaea leaves and 20g of olea europaea flowers in 500ml of hot water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids in a compost bin immediately.
Combine 150g of beeswax and 100g of olive oil in a double boiler and heat for 20 minutes.
Mix the infused liquid with 200g of aloe vera gel and 50g of the melted beeswax mixture thoroughly.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh allium sativum bulbs and wash them thoroughly in cold running water for 5 minutes.
Chop the bulbs into small pieces using a sharp knife and weigh out 10 grams.
Combine the chopped allium sativum with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax has melted.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under running cold water.
Grind 1 tablespoon of the washed zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the powdered zingiber officinale with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil have fully melted.
Stir the mixture well and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried echinacea angustifolia root and 50g of beeswax for the herbal infusion.

Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the herbal infusion and stir until melted.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh rhizomes of curcuma longa roots for making herbal creams.
Boil 2 cups of water and add the harvested rhizomes to extract their medicinal properties for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids to get a pure extract.
Mix 1 cup of coconut oil with 1 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of the pure extract.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180-190 fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including organic serenoa repens leaves dried at 50°c for 2 hours and virgin coconut oil in a 3:1 ratio.
Crush the dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Combine the crushed leaf powder with the virgin coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 10 minutes as it infuses for an additional 20 minutes to ensure optimal extraction of herbal properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours.
Grind dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and set aside.
Mix 20 grams of ground thymus vulgaris powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water and stir until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to create a gentle steam distillation process and extract essential oils.
Allow the cooled mixture to thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to glass jars and storing in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and equipment by submerging them in boiling water for at least 15 minutes.
Combine 250ml of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted and smooth.
Add 20 drops of eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 65°c before pouring into sterilized jars and sealing tightly.
Allow the creams to set for at least 30 minutes at room temperature before storing in a cool dark place.

Combine the dried leaves in a heat-resistant glass container with 100 milliliters of olive oil.
Steam-distill the mixture at 100 degrees celsius for two hours to obtain essential oil.
Mix one teaspoon of the obtained essential oil with 50 grams of beeswax and five tablespoons of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler and stir until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius before pouring into containers for later use.

remedy_properties: Tea tree oil: Has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Aloe vera: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe itching and reduce redness associated with ringworm.
Oregano oil: Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help treat skin infections caused by fungi, bacteria, or viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: Contain tannins and phenolic compounds that exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, making them effective against ringworm-causing pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tea leaves and dry them under shade to preserve their potency.
Infuse the dried tea leaves in hot water to create a strong tea decoction.
Strain the tea decoction and mix it with coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat until the beeswax melts and the ingredients combine smoothly.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which target the fungus that causes this skin infection. The cream's active compounds, such as beta-santalol and alpha-santalol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin. Additionally, sandalwood's antimicrobial properties help prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate ringworm symptoms. By applying the cream directly to affected areas, individuals can alleviate itching, redness, and scaling associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sandalwood contains compounds like alpha-santalol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, including those that cause ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: The essential oils present in sandalwood, such as beta-santalol, possess anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, helping to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with ringworm infections.

Antibacterial properties: Sandalwood's antibacterial compounds, like geraniol and linalool, can help combat secondary bacterial infections that often accompany ringworm, promoting faster healing and preventing further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sandalwood plant contain a high concentration of anti-fungal compounds, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: The barks of the sandalwood plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including ringworm, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried santalum album powder from a reputable supplier.
Combine the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes to incorporate air.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and seal tightly, allowing it to mature for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with ringworm because of its potent anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The boswellic acids in frankincense cream reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, allowing for faster healing and relief. Additionally, the cream's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of fungal infections, such as Trichophyton and Epidermophyton, which cause ringworm. By targeting both the symptoms and underlying causes of the infection, frankincense creams provide an effective natural treatment option for ringworm.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting a faster healing process.

Antifungal properties: The antifungal properties of frankincense, particularly the presence of incensole acetate, inhibit the growth of fungal species that cause ringworm, preventing infection spread and promoting recovery.

Wound-healing properties: Frankincense cream's wound-healing compounds, such as alpha-boswellic acid, enhance collagen synthesis, improve tissue regeneration, and reduce scarring, accelerating the healing process for ringworm lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: Contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing in ringworm-infected skin.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried boswellia serrata powder into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground boswellia powder with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2 drops of tea tree essential oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets to the mixture, stirring until the beeswax melts for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool at room temperature for 20 minutes before whipping with a hand mixer for 30 seconds.
Transfer the cream to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which are able to effectively target and eliminate fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The cream's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, work by disrupting the growth and spread of fungal cells, reducing symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a faster recovery from ringworm infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Ceylon cinnamon's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections like ringworm, preventing the growth and spread of the fungus.

Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams' anti-inflammatory properties soothe and reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and alleviating discomfort.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate the healing process and prevent scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which is beneficial for treating ringworm lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 100 degrees fahrenheit before adding 10 grams of aloe vera gel and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in an airtight container for at least 24 hours to allow for proper curing before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including salicylic acid and tannins, work together to soothe and calm affected skin, reducing redness and itching associated with the fungal infection. Additionally, witch hazel's natural antifungal compounds help combat the growth of ringworm fungus, promoting healthy healing and preventing further spread of the condition. This makes American witch hazel creams a popular and effective treatment option for those suffering from ringworm.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat the fungal infection causing ringworm, inhibiting its growth and promoting wound healing.

Phenolic Compounds: Phenolic compounds in Witch Hazel exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections, allowing for a more comfortable treatment experience.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Witch Hazel have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the infection from spreading and promote tissue repair, facilitating faster recovery from ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have antimicrobial properties that help to combat fungal infections like ringworm, allowing them to be effective in reducing symptoms and promoting healing.
Buds: The buds contain tannins, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry hamamelis virginiana leaves, collecting 2 cups of dried material for use.
Combine the dried hamamelis virginiana leaves with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan, heating mixture over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, allowing the hamamelis virginiana to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, retaining the infused oil and discarding solids after 1 hour.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter, heating the blend in a double boiler for 10 minutes to create a creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for ringworm are topical treatments that help alleviate symptoms and accelerate healing of ringworm infections. They work by reducing inflammation, promoting skin regeneration, and fighting fungal infections. The best herbal creams for ringworm include Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Azadirachta indica, Curcuma longa, Allium sativum, Zingiber officinale, Syzygium aromaticum, Aloe barbadensis, and Origanum vulgare. If left untreated, ringworm can cause significant discomfort, emotional distress, and social isolation, leading to missed work or school days, and impacting daily life and productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and pityriasis versicolor. These conditions are caused by different species of fungi but share similar symptoms like redness, itching, and scaly rashes. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and lavender oil have been found to be effective in treating these fungal infections, providing relief from itching and inflammation while promoting skin healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with ringworm is a blend of Tea Tree oil, Neem oil, and Aloe Vera gel. Tea Tree oil's antifungal properties combat the fungal infection, while Neem oil's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties reduce itching and promote healing. Aloe Vera gel soothes and moisturizes the affected area, preventing further irritation. Apply a mixture of equal parts Tea Tree oil and Neem oil to the affected area, followed by a layer of Aloe Vera gel for optimal healing and comfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, specifically its high concentration of tea tree oil. This essential oil has been shown to effectively combat fungal infections, including those caused by ringworm. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, work to reduce inflammation, prevent fungal growth, and promote healing. As a result, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can provide effective relief from ringworm symptoms, promoting the skin's natural healing process and reducing the risk of further infection.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent exhibits antifungal properties, which help inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm.
Limonene: As a volatile oil, limonene contributes to the antifungal and antibacterial effects that aid in treating and preventing the spread of ringworm.
Catechol: This phenolic compound displays antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Melaleuca alternifolia in creams for ringworm is the leaves, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, particularly tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties.
Barks: The barks are also commonly used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which helps to combat fungal infections such as ringworm.
Flowers: The flowers are sometimes used in creams for ringworm, as they contain tea tree oil, although to a lesser extent than the leaves and barks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and roots from a reputable supplier or wildcrafted source carefully.
Steep 50 grams of the dried plant material in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes slowly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids completely.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax thoroughly.
Combine 100 milliliters of the infused liquid with 20 grams of the melted beeswax mixture and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil thoroughly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, exhibit strong inhibitory effects against fungal pathogens, including Trichophyton, the primary cause of ringworm. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the affected skin, promoting a conducive environment for the skin's natural healing process and reducing the risk of further infection. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from the symptoms of ringworm, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections such as ringworm.
Flavonoids: These compounds exhibit antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities, contributing to the healing and soothing of affected skin and reducing the severity of ringworm symptoms.
Carotenoids: Their antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, promote wound healing, and fight off fungal infections, ultimately aiding in the recovery from ringworm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Seeds: They are used due to their saponin content, which has antifungal properties that can help alleviate ringworm symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3 for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the plant material.
Melt 10% beeswax and 90% infused oil in a double boiler at a temperature of 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Whip the melted mixture with a hand mixer until it has doubled in volume and becomes thick and fluffy.
Pour the whipped mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The gel from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. It also has the ability to inhibit the growth of fungal spores, which can help to clear up ringworm lesions. Additionally, Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to keep the skin hydrated and healthy, promoting a faster recovery from ringworm infections. This makes Aloe vera creams a popular natural remedy for ringworm treatment.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, thereby alleviating symptoms of ringworm.
Glucomannans: Possess antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi causing ringworm, reducing the severity of the infection.
Saponins: Have antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help to combat the fungal infection and promote healing of affected skin areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera due to their high concentration of soothing and antifungal compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to combat ringworm.
Gel (derived from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is widely used in creams for ringworm as it provides anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in the healing process.

Gel (derived from Leaves) is replaced with Aloe vera Extract (derived from Leaves): Aloe vera extract is commonly used in creams for ringworm as it contains a concentrated mixture of active compounds that help to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather aloe vera gel from mature plants harvested at dawn when it is most potent.
Measure 1/2 cup of distilled water and heat it to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 15 minutes.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler and melt them.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 teaspoons of aloe vera gel to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify completely in 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams helps with ringworm because they contain the active compound neem, which possesses antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's ability to inhibit fungal growth and kill bacteria helps to combat the underlying causes of ringworm, promoting a faster healing process. Additionally, neem's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm, making it a popular natural remedy for this common skin condition. Regular application of Azadirachta indica creams can lead to a complete recovery from ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds have antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of the ringworm-causing fungus Trichophyton spp., thereby facilitating the recovery of infected skin.
Phenolics: Phenolic compounds exhibit potent antiseptic and antifungal activities, which help to combat the fungal infection, reduce inflammation, and promote healing of the affected skin.

Sesquiterpenes: These compounds exhibit antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms, prevent secondary infections, and promote the recovery of the infected skin.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain the highest concentration of neem oil, which is the primary active ingredient used to treat ringworm due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are rich in neem oil and other bioactive compounds that help in treating ringworm, and are often used as an alternative to seeds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of azadirachta indica leaves and flowers, clean them thoroughly and dry them in shade for 3 days.
Grind 100 grams of dried leaves and flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine 500 grams of coconut oil, 100 grams of beeswax, and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the azadirachta indica powder to the oil mixture and heat it at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2 hours stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool and thicken for 4 hours before transferring it into containers for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, curcumin, inhibits the growth of fungal spores, reducing the severity of ringworm symptoms. Additionally, curcuma longa's anti-inflammatory properties soothe itchy and irritated skin, promoting faster healing and recovery. Topical application of curcuma longa creams directly targets the affected area, providing localized relief and supporting the body's natural healing processes to combat ringworm infections. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They exhibit antifungal properties, which help in inhibiting the growth and spread of ringworm-causing fungi.
Volatile oils (sesquiterpenes): They possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties that aid in reducing the infection and promoting healing.
Ferulic acid (phenolic compound): It exhibits potent antifungal activity and helps in preventing the re-infection of ringworm.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and heal the affected skin.
remedy_recipe: Grate 100 grams of fresh curcuma longa roots using a fine grater to obtain a smooth paste.
Combine the grated curcuma longa paste with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan over medium heat.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 30 minutes to 1 hour or until it thickens into a paste.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with the cooled curcuma longa paste in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.
Add 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well to enhance skin nourishment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which combat the fungal infection that causes the condition. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin and diallyl sulfide, have been shown to inhibit the growth of dermatophytes, the fungi responsible for ringworm. By applying garlic cream to the affected area, the fungal infection can be reduced, promoting healing and preventing the spread of the condition. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat ringworm and other skin fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Exhibits antifungal properties, effectively inhibiting the growth and spread of the fungal infection causing ringworm.
Sulfur-containing compounds (e.g., diallyl trisulfide): Display antimicrobial activity, targeting the fungal cells and preventing them from infecting healthy skin cells.
Quercetin: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, reducing redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm while also inhibiting the growth of the fungal infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most used parts of Allium sativum for making creams to treat ringworm are its Rhyzomes, as they contain sulfur compounds that have antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: The second most used part is the Leaves, which are also rich in sulfur compounds and other bioactive compounds that help to combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Roots: The third most used part is the Roots, which contain similar sulfur compounds and other active ingredients that contribute to the antifungal and antibacterial properties of the creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of allium sativum bulbs at the end of their growing season when tops have died back.
Steam the bulbs for 30 minutes to soften the outer skin and make peeling easier.
Peel the steamed bulbs and blend 1 cup of them with 1 cup of carrier oil in a blender.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Whip 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter in a separate bowl to thicken the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection responsible for ringworm. The cream's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, thereby speeding up the healing process. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with ringworm, making it an effective natural remedy for treating this common skin condition. Its soothing effects also promote faster recovery and skin regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the fungal infection and reduce redness and itching associated with ringworm.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating ringworm by inhibiting fungal growth and alleviating symptoms.

Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds have potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to combat the fungal infection, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing in ringworm patients.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are used because they contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.

Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that help in treating ringworm.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and treat ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried zingiber officinale root in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids to obtain the liquid extract.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of the zingiber officinale extract in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens.
Pour the thickened mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum creams helps with ringworm because they possess antifungal and antibacterial properties that target the fungal infection responsible for ringworm. The active compounds in these creams, including caryophyllene and eugenol, inhibit the growth of the fungus, thereby promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Syzygium aromaticum creams help soothe the affected skin, making it an effective and natural remedy for ringworm treatment. This results in faster recovery and reduced scarring.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the fungal load and prevent the spread of ringworm.
Eugenol: This phenolic compound has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the affected area, reduce inflammation, and prevent the growth of the fungal infection.
Linalool: This terpene has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms, prevent secondary infections, and promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are used to make creams for ringworm due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat the infection.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for ringworm due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and heal the affected skin.
Flowers: They are used to make creams for ringworm due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help reduce the severity of the infection and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10g of dried syzygium aromaticum, 5g of beeswax, 10g of shea butter, 5g of coconut oil.
Combine 10g of dried syzygium aromaticum with 20ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Mix 5g of beeswax, 10g of shea butter, and 5g of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Combine the strained syzygium aromaticum extract with the beeswax mixture and blend until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm. The anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera also help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and itching associated with ringworm. Additionally, Aloe vera's hydrating properties promote wound healing, making it a popular natural remedy for treating ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected skin, thereby aiding in the treatment of ringworm.
Saponins: Possess antimicrobial properties that help to kill the fungus causing ringworm, thus preventing its spread and promoting recovery.
Phenolic compounds: Have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of ringworm symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their rich concentration of anti-inflammatory and antifungal compounds, making them effective in treating ringworm.
Gel extracted from Leaves: The clear gel inside the leaves is used because it contains soothing properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected skin.
Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract is used due to its antifungal properties that help to combat fungal infections, a primary cause of ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of aloe barbadensis gel from 2 mature leaves using a blender to puree the flesh.
Combine 50g of beeswax 20g of coconut oil 10g of shea butter 5g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes to melt the beeswax completely.
Add the aloe barbadensis gel to the melted mixture and stir well to combine the ingredients evenly.
Pour the mixture into a mold and refrigerate for 30-40 minutes to allow it to set completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which are derived from the herb's essential oils. The primary active compound, carvacrol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause ringworm. Additionally, Origanum vulgare has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the affected skin. This natural remedy can aid in reducing the severity and duration of ringworm infections, promoting healthy skin and a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Exhibits antifungal properties, helping to inhibit the growth and spread of ringworm-causing fungi.
Caryophyllene oxide: Displays potent antifungal activity, which contributes to the reduction of fungal infections associated with ringworm.
Carvacrol: Shows antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to combat the underlying fungal and bacterial infections that cause ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Origanum vulgare for creams against ringworm, as they contain high concentrations of essential oils with antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
Stems: Stems of Origanum vulgare are used in creams due to their ability to inhibit fungal growth and promote wound healing.
Roots: Roots of the plant are used in creams to leverage their antifungal properties, which help in treating ringworm infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of origanum vulgare flowers and leaves and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Infuse 20g of dried origanum vulgare in 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Combine 5g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Blend the infused oil with the melted wax mixture and stir in 10g of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.
